headline: WEDNESDAY OPEN — Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over' late Tuesday after Strait-of-Hormuz tanker strikes; Brent +6.3% to $78.80, WTI +6.4% to $75.00; Dow futures -488 pts (-0.95%), S&P 500 futures -0.61% (-46 pts), Nasdaq futures -0.77% (-225 pts); European bourses gap down hard (DAX -2.4%, CAC -2.2%, FTSE -1.7%); Samsung -6.3% / SK Hynix -5.7% in Seoul; AMD -6.5%, INTC -9.7%, SPCX -6.8% in Tuesday-close chip-cohort selloff
summary: Speaking on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Ankara late Tuesday July 7, President Trump said the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is 'over' after both sides traded strikes near the Strait of Hormuz on three commercial tankers. Oil futures gap higher into the Wednesday open — Brent +6.3% to $78.80, WTI +6.4% to $75.00 (from the ~$69-70 stability band held since the mid-June MoU). Equity futures are broadly red: Dow -488 pts (-0.95%), S&P 500 -0.61%, Nasdaq -0.77%. European majors sold hard overnight: DAX -2.4%, CAC 40 -2.2%, FTSE 100 -1.7%. In Asia the chip cohort compounded Monday's Samsung-triggered valuation reset: Samsung Electronics -6.3%, SK Hynix -5.7%. Yesterday's US close was already the deepest chip-cohort down day of the quarter: INTC -9.7% to $110, AMAT -10% to $532, AMD -6.5% (Cash close ~$508 area), SPCX -6.8% on its Nasdaq-100 inclusion day. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE-TO-NEUTRAL near-term for the semi cohort (NVDA/AMD/AVGO/INTC/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML) as the crude spike layers macro-risk-off on top of the 'peak-cycle memory' selling that started Tuesday; (2) POSITIVE for defense-adjacent and integrated-energy stubs not in House coverage; (3) SUPPLY-CHAIN OVERHANG for TSM / Samsung / SK Hynix if the Strait of Hormuz situation escalates (energy input costs and shipping risk into Korea/Taiwan fabs); (4) 'Buy-the-dip' template argued by Wells Fargo / BofA (see BofA NVDA note below) means dispersion likely — the House coverage names with the cleanest 2027 EPS revision paths (NVDA, ETN, GEV, VRT, CEG) probably re-rally first if the situation stabilizes.

headline: NVDA — Bank of America reiterates Buy Wednesday July 8 morning, frames the pullback as 'enhanced Buy opp'ty for a unique, durable growth franchise now trading at a 7-yr low 18x forward PE'; NVDA -1.7% pre-market to $193.68 on the Iran-tape drag; note follows Goldman's Monday $640 AMD PT reset and Bernstein's Monday $2,623 ASML PT reset — the sell-side is publicly buying the semi cohort into weakness
summary: Bank of America's Vivek Arya reiterated Buy on Nvidia Wednesday July 8 pre-open with commentary framing the sell-off as a valuation opportunity: 'We strongly disagree with the EPS discount and see it as an enhanced Buy opp'ty for a unique, durable growth franchise now trading at a 7-yr low 18x forward PE.' Working target range stays near $275 (prior BofA reset in mid-May was $320 on the $1.7T AI-datacenter TAM framework). NVDA -1.7% pre-market to $193.68 on the Iran ceasefire tape combined with Tuesday's chip-cohort selloff. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECT POSITIVE for NVDA at the sell-side conviction floor — the '18x' framing is the cleanest bull-case anchor for the Aug/Sep FQ2 print set-up; (2) POSITIVE by extension for AMD (Goldman $640 Monday) and INTC as the semi cohort sell-side chorus turns constructive into the pullback; (3) The 'enhanced Buy opp'ty' language explicitly targets sponsor-mandate flows rather than retail — a signal the desks are advising re-adds not lightening; (4) MARGIN OF SAFETY for the House NVDA overweight is now materially higher than at the June $215 highs — the setup echoes late-April 2025 pre-Blackwell.

headline: SEMI COHORT — Tuesday close was the deepest chip-cohort down day of the quarter: INTC -9.7% ($110), AMAT -10% ($532), AMD -6.5% (~$508), SOXX -6%; Deutsche Bank called Samsung's Q2 preliminary print 'only 6% ahead of estimates' as the trigger; AMD trailing P/E now ~208x and AMAT guiding '30%+' 2026 growth, framing why even record beats aren't clearing sell-side hurdle rates
summary: Tuesday July 7 US session — chip cohort took the deepest single-day hit of the quarter as Samsung's Q2 preliminary print, while a 19x YoY OP surge to KRW 89.4T, was called 'only 6% ahead of consensus' by Deutsche Bank — enough to trigger profit-taking against the fresh Q2-highs the SOXX had put in on Monday. Cash close moves: INTC -9.7% to $110, Applied Materials -10% to $532, AMD -6.5% to ~$508, iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) -6% to $544. Deutsche Bank note framed the setup as valuation-vulnerable rather than fundamental — AMD carries a trailing P/E of ~208x and AMAT is guiding '30%+' revenue growth for 2026, both leaving 'little room for disappointment' on the incremental data-point. Dow closed at a record separately, VIX only at 15.81 — the sell was chip-cohort-specific. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE-TAILWIND for the equipment complex (AMAT / LRCX / KLAC / ASML / 8035.T / ENTG) — if Samsung's beat isn't the clearing datapoint, the group needs a sequential bank raise from MU (fiscal Q4 print later this quarter) or TSM (July 17 print) as the next tell; (2) NEGATIVE-TAILWIND for AMD after the Goldman $640 upgrade was faded within one session — the $700 Cantor top-of-Street PT is now the sell-side ceiling not floor; (3) NEUTRAL-POSITIVE for NVDA at 18x forward vs. AMD 208x trailing — the cohort dispersion inside semis is now the trade, not directional cohort exposure; (4) NEUTRAL for MU as Burry's July 2 $1,051 short print (closed $975 same day) becomes the reference bear anchor.

headline: CRWV — Galaxy Digital (GLXY) delivers Helios data-center campus Phase I to CoreWeave on schedule Monday July 6: 200 MW gross power / 133 MW critical IT load activated under 15-year lease; full Phase I-III lease covers 526 MW crit-IT (of 800 MW gross approved) targeting >$1B avg annual revenue; rent commencement began in Q2 2026 — first quarter of hard revenue on the CRWV backlog conversion story
summary: Galaxy Digital (GLXY) announced Monday July 6 that Helios data-center campus Phase I is now delivered on schedule, activating approximately 200 MW of gross power and 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave (CRWV) under the 15-year lease agreement disclosed prior. Rent commencement under Phase I began Q2 2026, converting the deal from construction spend to revenue for both counterparties. Across Phases I-III, CRWV has committed to 526 MW of critical IT load — the full 800 MW of gross approved capacity at the West Texas site — with the framework expected to generate >$1B of average annual revenue with two five-year renewal options. Site was originally acquired from Argo Blockchain for $65M in December 2022 — first tangible bitcoin-mine-to-AI-infrastructure conversion at scale. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECT POSITIVE for CRWV — the milestone hardens the '3.5 GW contracted / 1 GW active / 8 GW target by 2030' visibility framework and materially de-risks the 2026 revenue guide against the securities-class-action overhang that alleges CRWV concealed data-center construction delays; (2) POSITIVE for the neocloud framing — Helios shifting from capex to opex on schedule is the counter-datapoint against the 'delays' narrative that took CRWV -31% over the past month; (3) NEUTRAL-POSITIVE for hyperscaler-adjacent power infrastructure (GEV / ETN / VRT) that supplies the electrification content going into 133 MW critical-IT loads; (4) The 15-year duration + 5-year options template is the 'IRR floor' comparable that CRWV's next Wall Street note will benchmark to.

headline: GOOGL — Alphabet joins RWE, XTX Ventures, East X Ventures leading €411M ($469M) Series into Munich-based Proxima Fusion at €2.4B ($2.7B) post-money Tuesday July 7; largest single fusion round in Europe on record; capital funds 'Alpha' net-energy stellarator demonstrator near Munich; extends Google's fusion-plus-nuclear buildout book (Kairos SMR + TAE Technologies + now Proxima) to three named bets
summary: Proxima Fusion, the Munich-based stellarator fusion startup, announced Tuesday July 7 that it has closed a €411M ($469M) financing round at a €2.4B ($2.7B) post-money valuation — the largest private fusion round in European history and among the largest in European tech overall this year. Round was led by XTX Ventures and East X Ventures, with Google (Alphabet) and German utility RWE joining as strategic investors alongside KfW Capital, SPRIND, Burda Principal Investments, Plural, UVC Partners, Balderton, Cherry Ventures, DST Global Partners, Brevan Howard Macro Venture, Lightspeed, and other existing backers. Capital is earmarked for 'Alpha' — a net-energy stellarator demonstrator planned near Munich, intended to prove out the core physics + engineering integration ahead of a commercial pilot plant. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECT POSITIVE for GOOGL as the fusion-adjacent optionality book adds a third named bet (Kairos Power SMR, TAE Technologies tokamak, now Proxima stellarator) — the framing is diversified reactor-design exposure across the entire 'clean firm power' technology map; (2) NEUTRAL for the SMR complex (SMR/OKLO/CCJ/LEU) — fusion at 2030+ isn't a near-term substitution risk for fission commercialization on the AI-datacenter clock (2027-2030); (3) POSITIVE for the ex-US fusion tracker as a Europe-listed comp inside the House fusion_tracker.json; (4) The RWE presence in the syndicate hints at a future PPA offtake framework Europe-side — the utility parallel to the Meta/TerraPower/Vistra/Constellation US template.

headline: AMZN read-through — Blue Origin closes first-ever outside funding at $10B / $130B post-money Wednesday July 8 morning; Coatue Management leads with ~$4B commitment; Bezos personally contributes $2B; balance ~$4B from other institutional LPs; comps the post-IPO SpaceX $1.75T mark and telegraphs a Blue Origin IPO path 12-24 months out
summary: Blue Origin, the Jeff Bezos space company founded September 2000, disclosed Wednesday July 8 the first outside funding round in its 25-year history: $10B raise at a $130B post-money valuation. Coatue Management leads with a ~$4B commitment; Bezos personally contributes $2B; balance ~$4B allocated to unnamed institutional LPs. Round follows June's SpaceX IPO at $86B raised / $1.75T market cap and is being read on the tape as the setup for a Blue Origin IPO 12-24 months out. Capital is earmarked for New Glenn cadence + New Armstrong crewed-lunar program + Kuiper LEO constellation supply expansion. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEUTRAL-POSITIVE for AMZN — Blue Origin is technically independent of Amazon but Bezos remains the controlling shareholder, and the $130B private mark is a positive mark-to-market on the Kuiper LEO constellation (AMZN-financed) that competes with SpaceX Starlink; (2) NEGATIVE-DIRECTIONAL for SPCX at the margin — Blue Origin adds credible large-institutional-backed launch competition into the segment SpaceX has dominated on cadence; SPCX -6.8% Tuesday on Nasdaq-100 debut compounds the setup; (3) POSITIVE for the private-market space complex — Blue Origin's raise increases syndicate appetite for the next tier of pre-IPO space names (Firefly, Impulse, Stoke, etc.) though none are in House coverage.

headline: INTC / TSM read-through — Mizuho reiterates Outperform on SpaceX (SPCX) Wednesday July 8 pre-open, bullish on the $55B Texas chip-foundry plan disclosed in May 2026: cites 'supply-chain sovereignty' + 'margin-stack capture' as the strategic case; Musk-controlled foundry into full Tesla + SpaceX + xAI internal demand becomes a credible incremental competitor to Intel Foundry Services and, longer-term, to TSMC's US Arizona output
summary: Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh reiterated Outperform on SpaceX (SPCX) Wednesday July 8 morning, expressly endorsing the company's plan (disclosed May 2026) to build a $55B semiconductor foundry in Texas jointly with Tesla — with Musk-controlled leading-edge internal demand as the anchor customer base. Mizuho's two-word strategic framing: 'supply-chain sovereignty' + 'margin-stack capture.' The Tesla+SpaceX+xAI captive-demand pool is estimated at >20% of leading-edge foundry demand on the Musk-controlled entity roll-up by 2030, and would give SpaceX the option to eventually offer merchant capacity to third parties on the same node. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE-DIRECTIONAL for INTC — the Intel Foundry Services thesis (external customers absorbing 18A/14A capacity) faces a credible new Texas-based competitor with subsidized government interest (Section 232 alignment) and Musk-scale internal demand book; (2) NEGATIVE-DIRECTIONAL, longer-tenor, for TSM — Musk-controlled foundry captures the incremental 'US sovereignty' foundry TAM that TSMC Arizona was positioning to serve; (3) POSITIVE for AMAT / LRCX / KLAC / ASML — a fifth leading-edge foundry buyer (post-Samsung / Intel / TSMC / Rapidus) is unambiguous incremental equipment-order pipeline; (4) NEUTRAL for NVDA / AMD — Musk foundry, if built, isn't merchant-open at scale until post-2030, well after the FY2027/28 GPU capex cycle we care about.

headline: SECTION 232 SEMI-DATACENTER REPORT — Commerce Sec. Lutnick's statutory deadline was July 1; as of Wednesday July 8 pre-open, no public release, no leak, no scheduled press briefing; seven-day overrun is now the longest post-deadline silence of any Trump-era Section 232 report, extending the binary overhang for NVDA/AMD/INTC/MU/TSM as the Iran-ceasefire risk-off tape lands on top
summary: No update to report but the silence itself is now material. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick's Section 232 datacenter-semiconductor market study was due to the President on July 1 per Trump's January 14 Proclamation 11002. As of the Wednesday July 8 US open, the report has not been publicly released, no leaked highlights, no press briefing scheduled, and no Commerce fact-sheet. Seven trading days past deadline is longer than any prior Trump-era Section 232 report has taken to become public — a pattern that historically signals either (a) an escalatory outcome being drafted for maximum-impact release or (b) a private continuation of the current 25% tariff regime. Coming on top of the Iran ceasefire collapse that has crude +6% and semis -6-10% in the Tuesday tape, the incremental Section 232 binary is now the second-biggest macro overhang on the group after energy prices. Read-through to House coverage: (1) BINARY OVERHANG for NVDA/AMD/INTC (25% tariff on advanced computing chips is the current rate — could go higher/broader/lower); (2) MATERIAL RISK for MU / TSM / Samsung / SK Hynix if a foreign-fab component is added — Lutnick's public statement referenced 'up to 100%' on Korea and Taiwan fabs not investing in US capacity; (3) CATALYST WATCH for the tape any hour this week — a Truth Social post, White House fact-sheet, or selective leak becomes a top-of-book event.
